name: Daily Wonder
description: Waldorf-rooted homeschool curriculum created by three experienced Waldorf teachers, integrating all academic subjects through story, hands-on activities, movement, music, and visual/practical arts in daily morning lesson plans
notes: Created by Canadian Waldorf teachers who built and ran a Waldorf school. Digital PDF curriculum with 10 units per year (15 lesson days + 5 flex days each). Each day includes opening verse, movement, math, recall/review, new material, bookwork, story, and closing verse. Daily time commitment is 1-2 hours. Includes access to Wonder Hub with resource videos and supportive community. Individual units available at $44 each to trial. Also offers supplementary ukulele, recorder, pentatonic flute, and handwork courses. Aligned to BC Ministry of Education learning standards (translatable to other provinces/states). Block-teaching model typical of Waldorf education.

name: All About Spelling
description: Multi-sensory, Orton-Gillingham-based spelling program with scripted lessons, letter tiles, flashcards, and mastery-based progression through seven levels
notes: From the same publisher as All About Reading. Seven mastery-based levels (not grade-locked) — most students start at Level 1 regardless of grade. Requires one-time purchase of Letter Tiles ($26) and Spelling Review Box ($18) used across all levels. Level 1 costs $65; Levels 2-3 are $70; Levels 4-7 are $50 each. Fully scripted teacher manual requires no advance prep. Spelling sessions are 15-20 minutes, 3-4 days/week. Students spell full sentences from Level 1, not just isolated words. Excellent for struggling spellers and students with dyslexia. Parent involvement required (not independent work). Student packet is partially consumable. One-year money-back guarantee. Placement test available on publisher's website.

name: Spelling Power
description: Comprehensive, all-in-one spelling curriculum covering 5,000 most-frequently-used words across all grade levels in a single book, using research-proven strategies and 15-minute daily sessions
notes: One book covers entire family from age 8 through adult — a one-time purchase that lasts a lifetime. Manual only is $65; Basic Pack (manual + 2 student record books + activity task cards) is $103; Deluxe Pack (everything + magnetic letter tiles + word bank box) is $178. Diagnostic placement test determines starting point across 11 levels and 47 word groups. Requires only 15 minutes daily — 5 minutes direct instruction plus 10 minutes student work. Endorsed by International Dyslexia Association. Over 250,000 families served since 1993. Multiple award winner (Practical Homeschooling, Cathy Duffy Top 100). Initial setup can feel overwhelming due to extensive introductory material, but Quick Start guide and DVD help. Student Record Books are consumable ($8-10 each).

name: Earthschooling
description: Award-winning, secular Waldorf curriculum integrating nature studies, storytelling, painting, handwork, Eurythmy, and academics through hands-on activities and natural rhythms, with over 100 hours of teacher support tutorials
notes: The first online Waldorf curriculum provider, operating since 1994. Three purchase tiers: Core Curriculum (PDFs/images/MP3s), Curriculum Package (adds teacher video tutorials for crafts, watercolor, chalk drawing), and Living Lessons (adds video of Waldorf teacher teaching your student). Kindergarten package is $200; upper grades run $250-325. Lifetime Family Package (PreK-G8) is $2,225 without Living Lessons or $4,500 with them. High school (G9-12) is a separate $750 add-on converted to Waldorf School Online. Includes Eurythmy (unique among homeschool curricula), French lessons, music, international/multi-cultural content. Provides alternate lesson blocks for cultural sensitivity (e.g., secular alternatives to Saints block). 30% discount for AU/CA/NZ families. Scholarships available (up to 50% off). Lifetime access includes all future updates. Weekly Q&A sessions with Waldorf teacher included. Accepted by universities for transcripts.

name: Torchlight Curriculum
description: Literature-based, secular humanist curriculum emphasizing diverse authors, Socratic discussion, critical thinking, and hands-on projects across language arts, social studies, science, art, and music
notes: Covers Pre-K through Level 5 (ages 4-14) plus individual Constellation Studies for older students. Does NOT include math or explicit reading/phonics instruction (after Pre-K) — families must supplement. PDF-only curriculum; books purchased separately or borrowed from library. Level Pre-K is $32; Levels K-2 are $40-42; Level 3 is $47; Level 4 is $47; Level 5 is $52 ($76 with art and finance bundle). 36 weeks of daily lessons per level. Strong emphasis on diverse literature and multicultural perspectives. Socratic-inspired discussion and inquiry-based learning throughout. Includes art, music, philosophy, game logic, and creative writing (NaNoWriMo novel project in Levels 4-5). Cathy Duffy Top 103 Pick. Explicitly secular humanist — addresses worldview topics from humanist perspective, not religiously neutral. Heavy reading load; significant parent involvement required for discussions and activities. Very affordable curriculum guide, but book costs can add $100-200 per level if not using library.
